Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that:
  • Listeners can follow the line of reasoning.
  • The organization, development, vocabulary, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information and evidence so listeners understand the main points and the way ideas are organized fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions in order to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to get help, learn new information, or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Confirm understanding of a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media by asking and answering questions about key details and requesting clarification if something is not understood.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand stories or information by asking and answering questions and asking for help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Continue a conversation through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can keep a conversation going by taking turns talking several times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others and taking turns speaking about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules for talking with others, like listening and taking turns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about kindergarten topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child joins in conversations with different people about kindergarten topics and stories in small or large groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write or dictate writing routinely for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write or tell stories and information for different reasons and audiences regularly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over both short and long periods for different reasons and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: Begins in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, interpretation, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses details from books or articles to support their thoughts, ideas, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: When conducting research, gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and websites, checks if the sources are trustworthy, and uses the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of books by a favorite author and express opinions about them).
description_plain: Your child takes part in group research and writing projects, like reading books by a favorite author and sharing opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child completes both short and longer research projects by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including collaboration with peers.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools with help from adults to create and share writing, sometimes working with friends, and can write by printing letters, writing in cursive, or typing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or other technology to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to use vocabulary appropriate for kindergarten.
description_plain: Your child can use words that are right for kindergarten level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Begins in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: Your child can listen to questions and ideas from friends and add more details to their writing with adult help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 1.
description_plain: Begins in grade 1.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: For poems, use rhyming words to create structure.
description_plain: Your child uses rhyming words to help make poems with a clear pattern.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a single event or experience, or several loosely linked events or experiences; sequence the narrative appropriately and provide a reaction to what it describes.
description_plain: Your child tells a story about one or more events using drawings, words, or writing, putting the events in order and sharing how they feel about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, well-chosen details, and well-structured sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that include interesting details and follow a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts that name and supply some information about a topic.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, telling, and writing to share facts about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces that tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book (e.g., My favorite book is...).
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, telling, and writing to say their opinion about a topic or book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can join group reading activities and understand what is being read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, procedures).
description_plain: With help, your child can find ways two books about the same topic are alike and different, like in pictures or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell why an author gives reasons to support ideas in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the text in which they appear (e.g., what person, place, thing, or idea in the text an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures in a book show people, places, or ideas from the words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Name the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each in presenting the ideas or information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can name who wrote and who drew the pictures in a book and know what each person does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can find the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don’t know in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and sees how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how two people, events, or ideas in a book are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main topic of a book and share important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or themes in a text, follows how they develop, and summarizes the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a text says directly and makes logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group reading and understand the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell how characters in stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable.
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear (e.g., what moment in a story an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures in a story show a moment or part of the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, explain that reading the cover or title page is how to find out who created a book; name the author and illustrator of a book and define the role of each in telling the story.
description_plain: With help, your child can find who made a book by looking at the cover or title page and can name the author and illustrator and what each does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's viewpoint or reason for writing changes the content and style of a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of texts and characteristics of their structure (e.g., story elements in books; rhyme, rhythm, and repetition in poems).
description_plain: Your child can recognize different kinds of texts and their parts, like stories having characters and poems having rhyme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text. (See kindergarten Language Standards 4–6 on applying knowledge of vocabulary to reading.)
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about words they don’t know when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can explain what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, and major events in a story.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is in the story, where it happens, and what big things happen, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.
description_plain: Your child can retell familiar stories and include important details, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important parts of a story, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a story says and makes logical guesses, using examples from the story to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read early-emergent-reader texts with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read simple books carefully and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that look alike by listening to the different sounds in the letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read common high-frequency words by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).
description_plain: Your child can read common words by sight, like 'the,' 'you,' and 'is.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds with common spellings (graphemes) for the five major vowels.
description_plain: Your child knows the long and short sounds for the five main vowels and their common spellings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary sound or many of the most frequent sounds for each consonant.
description_plain: Your child can say the main sounds for most letters, especially consonants.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonant-vowel-consonant, or CVC) words.
description_plain: Your child can say the beginning, middle, and ending sounds in simple three-letter words like 'cat' or 'dog.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can put together and break apart parts of words that rhyme and sound alike.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count and say the parts (syllables) in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say words that rhyme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands how words are made up of sounds and parts like syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child understands that words are separated by spaces when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child understands that spoken words match specific letters in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right and top to bottom.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized and what it looks like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, activities in the grade 1 curriculum, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., because) to signal simple relationships.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and activities, including simple connecting words like 'because' to show relationships.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (e.g., large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words and describing words by explaining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at home that are cozy).
description_plain: Your child can notice how words relate to real life, like pointing out cozy places at home.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., a duck is a bird that swims; a tiger is a large cat with stripes).
description_plain: Your child can explain what words mean by naming their group and important details, like saying a duck is a bird that swims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories (e.g., colors, clothing) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups, like colors or clothes, to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child shows they understand how words relate to each other and have different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can recognize common root words and their different forms, like 'look,' 'looks,' and 'looking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child uses common beginnings and endings of words to help figure out their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words by using context, word parts, and reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 2.
description_plain: Begins in grade 2.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write numerals up to 120; understand that numbers are also written as words; write words for numbers from one to ten.
description_plain: Your child can write numbers up to 120 and write the words for numbers from one to ten.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child can spell new words by sounding them out and using spelling rules they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child can spell common words correctly, including some tricky words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate individual words in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly in dates and when listing things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the names of months and people.
description_plain: Your child can capitalize the names of months and people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses periods, question marks, and exclamation points at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print legibly all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can write all uppercase and lowercase letters clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions, adjectives, adverbs, conjunctions, and articles.
description_plain: Your child can use common small words like prepositions, adjectives, adverbs, conjunctions, and articles in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns.
description_plain: Your child can use different kinds of pronouns, like I, my, and someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use common nouns, names, and words that show ownership in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in sentences to convey a sense of past, present, and future.
description_plain: Your child can use verbs to show actions happening in the past, present, or future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding that a question is a type of sentence.
description_plain: Your child understands that a question is a special kind of sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand simple and compound sentences.
description_plain: Your child can make simple and compound sentences and add more details to them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; retain and further develop language skills learned in previous grades.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can say complete sentences when it fits the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child adds drawings or pictures to help explain ideas, thoughts, and feelings when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly and using appropriate vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child describes people, places, things, and events with details, using clear words to share ideas and feelings.

Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that:
  • Listeners can follow the line of reasoning.
  • The organization, development, vocabulary, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information and evidence so listeners understand the main points and the way ideas are organized fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what someone says to learn more or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over both short and long periods for different reasons and readers.

description_plain: Your child uses details from books or articles to support their thoughts, ideas, and research.
