description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: When conducting research, gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and websites, checks if the sources are trustworthy, and uses the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of how-to books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).
description_plain: Your child can work with others to research and write, like reading how-to books and writing steps to do something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child completes both short and longer research projects by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With support, your child can use computers or other tools to write and share their work, learning to print, write cursive, or type.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or other technology to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to choose and use appropriate vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child can pick and use the right words when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Begins in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With guidance, your child focuses on a topic, listens to ideas from friends, and adds details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write with clear ideas and organization that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: For poems, use rhyming words and words that repeat long or short vowel sounds to create structure.
description_plain: Your child uses rhyming words and repeated vowel sounds to help create poems with a clear pattern.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in prose or poem form that recount two or more appropriately sequenced events or experiences, include some details about what happened or was experienced, use temporal words to signal order where appropriate, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories or poems that tell about two or more events in order, include details, use words that show time, and have an ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, well-chosen details, and well-structured sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that include interesting details and follow a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts that name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that name a topic, give some facts, and have a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces that introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that introduce a topic or book, state their opinion, give a reason, and end clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and comprehend informational texts exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 1.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand simple informational books with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: Your child can find simple ways two texts about the same topic are alike or different, like pictures or descriptions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the reasons an author gives to support ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a text to explain the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between information from pictures and information from words in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of a book like headings, tables of contents, and glossaries to find important facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand the meaning of words and phrases in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and sees how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main topic of a text and tell important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or themes in a text, follows how they develop, and summarizes the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a text says directly and makes logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read and comprehend literary texts representing a variety of genres, cultures, and perspectives and exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 1.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different kinds of stories from various cultures with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can tell how characters’ adventures and experiences are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable.
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a story to describe characters, places, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's viewpoint or reason for writing changes the content and style of a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify characteristics of common types of stories, including folktales and fairy tales.
description_plain: Your child can recognize common features of stories like folktales and fairy tales.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or help imagine senses like sight or sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can explain what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, places, and important events in a story using key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their central message or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories with important details and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a story says and makes logical guesses, using examples from the story to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and reads common tricky words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child reads words with endings that change their meaning, like -ed or -s.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child breaks two-syllable words into parts to read them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows that every syllable has a vowel sound and uses this to count syllables in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows that a silent e or vowel pairs can make a long vowel sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words that follow common spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read common two-letter sounds like ch, sh, and th.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can say all the individual sounds in a one-syllable word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can say the beginning, middle, and ending sounds in one-syllable spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say a one-syllable word by putting together sounds, including blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands spoken words, syllables, and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child knows what makes a sentence, like starting with a capital letter and ending with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works and the basic parts of books and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, activities in the grade 2 curriculum, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe.
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and activities, including describing words like adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child notices small differences in the meanings of similar verbs and adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to real-life things, like describing foods as spicy or juicy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use appropriately symbols related to grade-level content or commonly used in everyday life (e.g., $, ¢).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use symbols like $ and ¢ that they see in school and everyday life.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use appropriately abbreviations related to grade-level content or commonly used in everyday life (e.g., a.m., p.m.)
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use common abbreviations like a.m. and p.m. correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses glossaries and simple dictionaries, in print or online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).
description_plain: Your child guesses the meaning of compound words by knowing the meanings of the smaller words inside them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child uses the meaning of a root word to help understand new words with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new words by adding prefixes to words they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to figure out the meaning of new or tricky words when reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words by using context, word parts, and reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding that context determines whether the writer uses a numeral or a written number (e.g., numerals in 1 + 3 = 4, but written words in "When I was one, I was just begun, / When I was two, I was still quite new" from A. A. Milne's poem "Now We Are Six").
description_plain: Your child understands when to use numbers or words for numbers depending on the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other tools to check and fix spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they have learned to write new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes to make contractions and show ownership.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly in greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child knows to capitalize names of holidays, products, and places.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print upper- and lowercase letters legibly and fluently.
description_plain: Your child can write uppercase and lowercase letters clearly and smoothly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use the past tense of common irregular verbs correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns.
description_plain: Use reflexive pronouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns and frequently occurring irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use group nouns and irregular plural nouns correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs in sentences and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words and action words in sentences and choose the right one depending on what they want to say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences and choose among sentence types depending on the meaning to be conveyed.
description_plain: Your child can make and expand different kinds of sentences, like statements, questions, commands, and exclamations, depending on what they want to say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; retain and further develop language skills learned in previous grades.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing, building on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child speaks in complete sentences when needed to give details or explain something clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or descriptions of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can make audio recordings of stories or poems and add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story, recount an experience, or explain how to solve a mathematical problem with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences and using appropriate vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story, share an experience, or explain how to solve a math problem using clear facts and details, speaking clearly with good vocabulary.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that:
  • Listeners can follow the line of reasoning.
  • The organization, development, vocabulary, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information and evidence so listeners understand the main points and the way ideas are organized fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to better understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe important ideas or details from stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more explanation when they don’t understand what is being talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can join conversations by adding comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group talks, like waiting their turn, listening carefully, and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group conversations about grade 2 topics with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over both short and long periods for different reasons and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: Begins in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, interpretation, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses details from books or articles to support their thoughts, ideas, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report; record science observations).
description_plain: Your child works with others to research a topic by reading books and writing reports or recording observations.

subject: Language Arts
description: When conducting research, gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and websites, checks if the sources are trustworthy, and uses the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child uses computers or other digital tools to write and share their work, sometimes working with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child completes both short and longer research projects by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to choose and use appropriate vocabulary
description_plain: Your child shows they can pick and use the right words when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or other technology to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With support, your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by revising and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write with clear organization and details that fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: For poems, use words and phrases that form patterns of sounds (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, end rhymes, repeated sounds in words or lines) to create structure.
description_plain: Your child can use sound patterns like rhymes and repeated sounds to create poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in prose or poem form that recount a well-elaborated event or experience, or a set of events or experiences; include details and dialogue to show actions, thoughts, and feelings; use temporal words to signal order where appropriate; and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories or poems that tell about events with details, dialogue, order words, and a clear ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, well-chosen details, and well-structured sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that include interesting details and follow a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts that introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write short reports that introduce a topic, use facts to explain it, and end with a closing sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces that introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words (e.g., because, and, also) to connect opinion and reasons, and provide a concluding statement or section. In math, instead of writing opinions, students write or draw solutions to math word problems and present arguments to explain their thinking.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that say what they think about a topic or book, give reasons for their opinion using words like because and also, and finish with a closing sentence; in math, they can write or draw solutions to problems and explain their thinking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, mathematical, and technical texts, exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 2.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand second-grade informational texts about history, science, math, and technology on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare the main points from two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how reasons in a text support the author’s main points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures or diagrams help explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main purpose of a text, like what the author wants to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use text features like captions, bold words, and indexes to find important information quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in texts about grade 2 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and sees how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, mathematical ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multi-paragraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child finds the main topic of a text and the focus of each paragraph.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or themes in a text, follows how they develop, and summarizes the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a text says directly and makes logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend literary texts representing a variety of genres, cultures, and perspectives and exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 2.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand second-grade stories and poems from different cultures and types on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (e.g., Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more versions of the same story by different authors or from different cultures and notice how they are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable.
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in books or on screens to understand the characters, where the story happens, and what happens in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain what dialogue is and how it can reveal characters' thoughts and perspectives.
description_plain: Your child understands that dialogue is when characters talk and can explain how it shows what characters think and feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's viewpoint or reason for writing changes the content and style of a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is put together, explaining how the beginning starts the story and the ending finishes it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases (e.g., regular beats, alliteration, rhymes, repeated lines) supply rhythm and meaning in a story, poem, or song.
description_plain: Your child can explain how words and phrases like rhymes, repeated lines, or beats add rhythm and meaning to stories, poems, or songs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can explain what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters react to important events and challenges in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including fables and folktales from different cultures, and explain the main lesson or moral.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a story says and makes logical guesses, using examples from the story to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a tricky word to figure out or fix how to say or understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read second-grade texts out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read second-grade texts carefully and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can spot words that have unusual but common ways of matching letters to sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have common beginnings or endings added.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words with long vowel sounds that follow regular spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read vowel teams (two vowels that make one sound) in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words and phrases that fit their grade level, including words that show location and time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).
description_plain: Your child understands small differences in meaning between similar words that show feelings or how sure someone is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life situations, like describing friendly or helpful people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between the literal meaning and the figurative or hidden meaning of words and phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use appropriately symbols related to grade-level content or common in everyday life (e.g., <, >).
description_plain: Your child knows how to use symbols like < and > correctly in everyday situations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use appropriately abbreviations related to grade-level content or common in everyday life (e.g., N, S, E, W on a map).
description_plain: Your child knows how to use common abbreviations like N, S, E, W on maps correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses glossaries or simple dictionaries, in books or online, to find out what important words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).
description_plain: Your child uses the meaning of a root word they know to help understand new words with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (e.g., agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new words by adding prefixes or suffixes to words they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the rest of the sentence to help understand the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words by using context, word parts, and reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written English.
description_plain: Your child understands the differences between how we speak and how we write English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases to make their writing more interesting or clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spellings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling rules and patterns to help write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding that numerals used at the beginning of a sentence are written as words and capitalized (e.g., "Three pandas could be seen eating leaves high in the bamboo grove.").
description_plain: Your child knows to write numbers as words and capitalize them when they start a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (e.g., sitting, smiled, cries, happiness).
description_plain: Your child spells common words and words with endings like -ing or -ed correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use possessives.
description_plain: Form and use possessives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child uses commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in addresses.
description_plain: Use commas in addresses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the right words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write legibly and fluently by hand, using either printing or cursive handwriting.
description_plain: Your child writes neatly by hand, using either printing or cursive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns and the past tense of regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child uses plural nouns and past tense verbs correctly, including irregular forms.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns.
description_plain: Use abstract nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs and choose between them depending on what is to be modified and the overall meaning of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child uses words like bigger or biggest correctly to compare things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions and choose between them depending on the overall meaning of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child uses connecting words like and, but, or because correctly to make sentences clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs in the present, past, and future tenses and choose among them depending on the overall meaning of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child uses verbs in present, past, and future tenses correctly depending on the sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.7
description_plain: Your child makes sure subjects and verbs agree and pronouns match the nouns they replace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child writes complete sentences and can make them longer or rearrange them to make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; retain and further develop language skills learned in previous grades.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child speaks in full sentences when needed to explain or give more details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child makes interesting audio recordings of stories or poems that are easy to understand and adds pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic, text, or solution to a mathematical problem, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace and using appropriate vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child can clearly talk about a topic, story, or math problem using facts and details, speaking at a good pace with the right words.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that:
  • Listeners can follow the line of reasoning.
  • The organization, development, vocabulary, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information and evidence so listeners understand the main points and the way ideas are organized fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what a speaker says, adding details when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas and important details when listening to stories or information in different formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child explains their own ideas and what they understand during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand what others say, stay focused on the topic, and connect their ideas to what others have shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child will follow simple rules during discussions, like waiting their turn to speak, listening carefully, and talking one at a time about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading or studying the material and uses what they learned to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can join different kinds of group talks, listen to others, build on their ideas, and clearly share their own thoughts about grade 3 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over both short and long periods for different reasons and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: Begins in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, interpretation, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses details from books or articles to support their thoughts, ideas, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child remembers information from experiences or finds facts from books and websites, takes short notes, and organizes the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: When conducting research, gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and websites, checks if the sources are trustworthy, and uses the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child completes both short and longer research projects by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use a computer or tablet to write and share their work and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or other technology to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to choose and use appropriate vocabulary
description_plain: Your child can pick and use the right words to make their writing clear and interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English conventions (as described in Language Standards 1– 3 up to and including grade 3).
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar, punctuation, and spelling as expected for third grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: Your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes in a way that fits the purpose, audience, and type of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: For poems, use words and phrases that form patterns of sound (e.g., rhyme, repetition of sounds within words or within lines) to create meaning or effect.
description_plain: Your child uses rhymes and repeated sounds in poems to create meaning or effects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Provide a sense of closure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal order where appropriate.
description_plain: Your child uses words like first, next, and finally to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use figurative language to suggest images.
description_plain: Your child uses creative language to help readers picture what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences or events or show responses to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions of actions and feelings to show what happens or how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a speaker, narrator, and/or characters; organize an appropriate narrative sequence.
description_plain: Your child starts a story by introducing the setting and characters and organizes events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in prose or poem form to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, descriptive details, and clear sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories or poems that show events or experiences with good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, well-chosen details, and well-structured sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that include interesting details and follow a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear ending to their piece.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child uses words like also, and, and but to connect ideas in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child adds facts and details to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group-related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic and groups related information together, adding pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can write about their opinions on topics or stories and give reasons to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, mathematical, and technical texts, exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 3.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand informational texts like history, science, math, and technical writing on their own at a third-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
