description_plain: Your child explains how sentences and paragraphs in a text are connected, like showing cause and effect or order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

description_plain: Your child can use pictures, maps, numbers, and words in a text to understand important details like where, when, why, and how events happen.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the author of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

description: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show feelings, characters, or settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the opinions of a story’s narrator or characters.

description_plain: Your child understands how an author's viewpoint or reason for writing changes the content and style of a story or text.

description_plain: Your child can identify parts of a story like the problem and solution and explain how each part connects to what came before.

description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts

description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.

description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a story says and makes logical guesses, using examples from the story to explain their ideas.

description_plain: Your child explains simple comparisons like similes and metaphors in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct capitalization.

description_plain: Your child writes neatly by hand, either printing or cursive, and can sign their name in cursive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts

description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting research findings) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child knows when to speak formally, like in presentations, and when informal talk is okay, and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that:
  • Listeners can follow the line of reasoning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over both short and long periods for different reasons and readers.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to use general academic and domain-specific vocabulary appropriately.
description_plain: Your child uses school and subject-specific words correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English conventions.
description_plain: Your child follows standard English rules when writing.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, well-chosen details, and well-structured sequences.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly and groups related information in paragraphs, using headings or pictures to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section that relates to their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., for instance, in order to, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can connect their opinion and reasons using words like 'for example' or 'in addition.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons that are backed up with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped in paragraphs and sections to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, state their opinion, and organize their ideas in paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that share their point of view with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, mathematical, and technical texts, exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 4.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand informational texts about history, science, math, and technology on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak knowledgeably about the subject.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from two texts on the same topic to talk or write about it knowledgeably.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, timelines, animations, or interactive elements on webpages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in charts, graphs, or pictures and how it helps explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event and explain how they focus on different details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by time order, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to grade 4 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and sees how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, mathematical, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain what happened and why in history, science, math, or technical texts using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or themes in a text, follows how they develop, and summarizes the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text states explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child uses details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a text says directly and makes logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend literary texts representing a variety of genres, cultures, and perspectives and exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 4.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories from different genres and cultures on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare how similar themes and story patterns are shown in stories and myths from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable.
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between a written story or drama and its visual or oral presentation, identifying where the presentation reflects specific descriptions and directions in the written text.
description_plain: Your child can connect a story or play they read to how it looks or sounds in a show or presentation, pointing out where the show matches the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the points of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between stories told by a character in the story (first-person) and stories told by someone outside the story (third-person), and can compare these points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's viewpoint or reason for writing changes the content and style of a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences among prose, poetry, and drama and refer to the structural elements of each (e.g., paragraphs and chapters for prose; stanza and verse for poetry; scene, stage directions, cast of characters for drama) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how stories, poems, and plays are different and talk about parts like paragraphs in stories, stanzas in poems, and scenes or characters in plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including those that allude to significant characters found in mythology (e.g., Herculean); explain how figurative language (e.g., simile, metaphor) enriches a text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including those that refer to famous mythological characters, and explain how figurative language makes the text better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can explain what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, setting, or event in detail using specific parts from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, poem, or play by looking at details and can tell the story in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text states explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child uses details and examples from a text to explain what it says clearly and to make guesses about what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a story says and makes logical guesses, using examples from the story to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads stories and poems out loud accurately, with good speed and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level texts with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about letters, syllables, and word parts to read long or new words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition, and other logical relationships (e.g., however, although, nevertheless, similarly, moreover, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words that show ideas like contrast and addition, such as however, although, and moreover.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, homographs) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language like similes and metaphors in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use appropriately symbols related to grade-level content or common in everyday life, including symbols with multiple meanings (e.g., parentheses in mathematics and in writing, to measure angles and temperature).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use symbols like parentheses correctly in different subjects and everyday life.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and use appropriately abbreviations related to grade-level content or common in everyday life, including abbreviations derived from words or phrases in other languages (e.g., lb., oz., etc.).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and use common abbreviations, including those from other languages, correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources, both books and online, to find how to say and understand words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., photograph, photosynthesis).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., cause/effect relationships and comparisons in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the text, like cause and effect, to understand the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 5 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words by using context, word parts, and reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English (e.g., dialects, registers) used in stories, dramas, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can compare different ways English is spoken or written in stories and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.12
description_plain: Your child changes sentences by adding, combining, or shortening them to make writing clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-appropriate words correctly and use resources to help when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child can use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to show titles of books, stories, or other works.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no (e.g., Yes, thank you), to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence (e.g., It's true, isn't it?), and to indicate direct address (e.g., Is that you, Steve?).
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly with words like yes and no, in tag questions, and when directly speaking to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma after introductory words or phrases at the start of a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to separate items in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas to separate items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write legibly and fluently by hand, using either print or cursive handwriting; write their given and family name signature in cursive.
description_plain: Your child can write neatly and smoothly by hand, using print or cursive, and can sign their name in cursive.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use perfect verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can form and use perfect verb tenses like 'have walked' or 'had eaten' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use active and passive verbs, choosing between them depending on the overall meaning of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use active and passive verbs and choose the right one to match the meaning of the sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.11
description_plain: Your child notices and fixes mistakes when verb tenses change incorrectly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states, and conditions, choosing among verb tenses depending on the overall meaning of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use different verb tenses to show when things happen and choose the right tense for the meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; retain and further develop language skills learned in previous grades.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on skills learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, using formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information from stories read aloud or from different types of media like videos or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic, text, procedure, or solution to a mathematical problem, or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace and use appropriate vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child can clearly speak about a topic, story, or math problem in order, using facts and details, and use words that listeners understand.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that:
  • Listeners can follow the line of reasoning.
  • The organization, development, vocabulary, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information and evidence so listeners understand the main points and the way ideas are organized fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child summarizes what a speaker says and explains how the speaker supports their ideas with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information from a text read aloud or from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child understands the main ideas shared in a discussion and can make conclusions based on what was talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions during a discussion and add to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child follows the agreed rules during discussions and does the roles they are assigned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying the topic and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 5 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of discussions with others, listens to their ideas, and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over both short and long periods for different reasons and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support written analysis, reflection, and research, applying one or more grade 5 standards for Reading Literature or Reading Informational Text as needed.
description_plain: Your child can find and use information from stories or facts to support their writing and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, interpretation, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses details from books or articles to support their thoughts, ideas, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child remembers or finds important information from experiences, books, or the internet, summarizes it, and lists where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
