description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and websites, checks if the sources are trustworthy, and uses the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child completes both short and longer research projects by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including current web-based communication platforms, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of two pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write, share their work, and work with others, typing at least two pages in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or other technology to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to use general academic and domain-specific vocabulary appropriately.
description_plain: Your child uses general and subject-specific vocabulary correctly in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English conventions.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar, punctuation, and spelling in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas to fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: For poems, draw on characteristics of traditional poetic forms (e.g., ballads, couplets) or modern free verse from diverse cultures as models for writing.
description_plain: Your child can write poems using styles from different cultures, like ballads or free verse, as examples.

subject: Language Arts
description: For prose narratives, draw on characteristics of traditional or modern genres (e.g., tall tales, myths, mysteries, fantasies, historical fiction) from diverse cultures as models for writing.
description_plain: Your child can write stories using traditional or modern types like myths, mysteries, or fantasies from different cultures as models.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure appropriate to the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can end their stories with a clear conclusion that fits the events they wrote about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences or events precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words and details that appeal to the senses to describe experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to manage sequence.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques such as dialogue, description, and pacing to develop experiences or events or show responses to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use dialogue, descriptions, and pacing to develop stories and show how characters feel or react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a speaker, narrator, and/or characters; organize an appropriate narrative sequence.
description_plain: Your child can start stories by setting the scene and introducing characters or narrators in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in prose or poem form to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, descriptive details, and clear sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories or poems that share experiences or events using good storytelling techniques and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, well-chosen details, and well-structured sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that include interesting details and follow a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and special vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., in contrast, especially).
description_plain: Your child connects ideas using words like 'however' or 'especially' to show how information relates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, examples, or quotes to explain their topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically in paragraphs and sections; include text features (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, organizes information in paragraphs, and uses headings or pictures to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic and share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing sentence or paragraph that sums up their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., consequently, specifically).
description_plain: Your child connects their opinion and reasons using words like 'because' or 'therefore.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons in a clear order and supports them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped in paragraphs and sections to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic, states their opinion, and organizes ideas in paragraphs to explain their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces on topics or texts, giving reasons and information to support their view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, mathematical, and technical texts, exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 5.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand fifth-grade informational texts about history, science, math, and technology on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak knowledgeably about the subject.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from several books or articles about the same topic and use it to talk or write clearly about that topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child understands how an author uses reasons and facts to support their ideas and can point out which reasons go with which ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw on information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can find answers or solve problems quickly by using information from several books, websites, or other sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences among the points of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child can look at different stories about the same event and notice important similarities and differences in how people see it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how an author uses one or more structures (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, to present information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author organizes information in a text, like by telling events in order or showing cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to school subjects or topics they are learning about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and sees how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, mathematical, or technical text based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two or more people, events, or ideas are connected based on details in a history, science, math, or technical text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine one or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in a text, explain how details support those ideas, and tell the main points in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or themes in a text, follows how they develop, and summarizes the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase a text accurately when explaining what the text states explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can correctly quote or restate parts of a text when explaining what it says or making guesses based on it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a text says directly and makes logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend literary texts representing a variety of genres, cultures, and perspectives and exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 5.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories and poems from different cultures and types of writing that are suitable for fifth grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre (e.g., mysteries or adventure stories) on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories that are the same type, like mysteries or adventures, and talk about how they handle similar ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable.
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or beauty of a text (e.g., graphic novel; multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, poem).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures, videos, or other media add to the meaning, feeling, or beauty of a story, poem, or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described in a story, myth, poem, or drama.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view changes the way events are told in a story, poem, or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's viewpoint or reason for writing changes the content and style of a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters, scenes, or parts of a poem fit together to make the whole story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text; identify and explain the effects of figurative language such as metaphors and similes.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story and explain how things like metaphors and similes make the writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can explain what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., how characters interact).
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more characters, places, or events in a story by using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem by looking at details and can tell the story in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote or paraphrase a text accurately when explaining what the text states explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can accurately quote or restate parts of a story or poem when explaining what it says or making guesses about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a story says and makes logical guesses, using examples from the story to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read fifth-grade stories and poems out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read fifth-grade texts carefully and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use their knowledge of sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new, longer words correctly in and out of stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to figure out words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia components and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to make information clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate vocabulary, eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share ideas and findings with good reasons and details, using the right words, eye contact, volume, and clear speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker’s main points, decide if their reasons and evidence make sense, and notice if they use information that doesn’t fit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why information is shown in different ways and think about the reasons behind it, like social or political motives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child listens to new ideas from others and can change or explain their own views based on the evidence they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child asks questions that connect ideas from different speakers and answers others with relevant facts and thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group talks, helps make decisions, keeps track of goals and deadlines, and understands their role.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion. (See grades 6–8 Reading Standard 1 for more on the use of textual evidence.)
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses that information to think about and discuss ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on discipline-specific topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, builds on their ideas, and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long times for different subjects, purposes, and readers, allowing time to think and improve.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, interpretation, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses facts and details from informational texts to support their thinking, explanations, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: When conducting research, gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and online, checks if sources are trustworthy, and uses that information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child does short and longer research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking new related questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including current web-based communication platforms, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses technology and online tools to write, share, and clearly show how ideas and information connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable.
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that supports the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a style appropriate to audience and purpose (e.g., formal for academic writing).
description_plain: Your child keeps a writing style that fits the audience and purpose, like using formal language for school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas, concepts, or procedures.
description_plain: Your child uses different linking words to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples that fit well to develop their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; use paragraphs and sections to organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include text features (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child starts writing by clearly introducing the topic, organizing ideas with paragraphs and headings, and adding helpful pictures or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/ experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child writes informative or explanatory texts, like telling about history events, science experiments, or how things work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that clearly supports their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a style appropriate to audience and purpose (e.g., formal for academic writing).
description_plain: Your child will write in a way that fits the audience and purpose, like using formal language for school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses with precision to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims/critiques, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and precise words to connect ideas and explain how claims and evidence relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child will support their opinions with clear reasons and accurate facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims/critiques, and organize the reasons and evidence logically in paragraphs and sections.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main opinion, recognize other viewpoints, and organize reasons and evidence in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about specific subjects they are studying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend science/technical texts exhibiting complexity appropriate for the grade/course.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science and technical texts that are challenging for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares information from experiments, videos, or simulations with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, research-based opinions, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child combines information explained in words with pictures like charts or graphs to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze an author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child figures out why an author explains something, describes how to do something, or talks about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child looks at how an author organizes a text and how each part helps explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic vocabulary as well as symbols, notation, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6–8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child understands important academic and subject-specific words, symbols, and terms used in science or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multi-step procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child carefully follows step-by-step instructions when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or conclusions in a text and summarizes it accurately without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts, quoting or paraphrasing as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact details from science and technical texts to explain their ideas, either by quoting or putting the information in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend history/social studies texts exhibiting complexity appropriate for the grade/course.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history and social studies texts that are right for their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how a primary source and a secondary source on the same topic are related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and thoughtful judgments in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, charts, maps, or videos along with written information to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can find parts of a text that show the author's opinion or reason for writing, like strong words or missing facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally), including how written texts incorporate features such as headings.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text shares information, like in order or by comparing ideas, and notice features like headings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words used in history and social studies texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can identify the main steps in a process described in history or social studies, like how a law is made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in a source and give a clear summary that is different from their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, quoting or paraphrasing as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact parts of a text to support their understanding, by quoting or putting it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; independently research words and gather vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic and subject-specific words correctly and can research words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., stingy, scrimping, economical, unwasteful, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses the relationship between words, like cause and effect or part and whole, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., personification) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figures of speech, like when something is described as if it were a person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains phrases that mean more than their literal words, how words relate to each other, and small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word by looking at how it’s used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word or what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., audience, auditory, audible).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to help figure out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence or paragraph around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words by using context, word parts, and reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard or formal English in writing and speaking, determine their appropriateness for the intended purpose and audience, and make changes as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can notice when writing or speaking is different from formal English, decide if it's okay for the situation, and make changes if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain appropriate consistency in style and tone while varying sentence patterns for meaning and audience interest.
description_plain: Your child can keep a consistent style and tone in their writing while using different sentence types to keep the reader interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses the rules of language when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly, recognizing that some words have commonly accepted variations (e.g., donut/doughnut).
description_plain: Your child spells words correctly and knows that some words have common alternative spellings, like donut and doughnut.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child uses commas, parentheses, or dashes to add extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place or rearrange phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child can move phrases and clauses around in sentences and fix mistakes where parts of a sentence don’t clearly connect to the right word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general, how phrases and clauses differ, and how their use conveys a particular meaning in a specific written or spoken sentence.
description_plain: Your child can explain what phrases and clauses do in sentences, how they are different, and how they help give meaning in writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to communicate ideas clearly and to add variety to writing.
description_plain: Your child uses different kinds of sentences—simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex—to share ideas clearly and make writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; retain and further develop language skills learned in previous grades.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate vocabulary, eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas clearly and in order, using facts and details, while speaking with good eye contact and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that:
  • Listeners can follow the line of reasoning.
  • The organization, development, vocabulary, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information and evidence so listeners understand the main points and the way ideas are organized fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child identifies a speaker’s main argument and knows which claims have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child understands information presented in different ways, like pictures or numbers, and explains how it relates to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain different viewpoints by summarizing what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer detailed questions to add to a discussion about a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group talks, sets goals and deadlines, and knows their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group discussions, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revision.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over both short and long periods for different reasons and readers.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can use information from stories or informational texts to support their writing, like analysis or research.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can use computers and online tools to write and share their work and work with others, typing at least three pages in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or other technology to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to select accurate vocabulary.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right words to make their writing clear and accurate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English conventions.
description_plain: Your child can use correct English grammar and writing rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows naturally from the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, figurative and sensory language, and techniques such as personification (e.g., "the fog crept in") to convey experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and descriptive words, details, and creative language like personification to show experiences or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child will use dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to make stories and characters more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an appropriate narrative sequence.
description_plain: Your child starts a story by setting the scene, introducing characters or narrator, and organizing events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that clearly show events or experiences using good details and a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, well-chosen details, and well-structured sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that include interesting details and follow a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a style appropriate to audience and purpose (e.g., formal for academic writing).
description_plain: Your child can keep a writing style that fits the audience and purpose, like using formal language for school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to show how ideas and concepts connect.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic and organize ideas in paragraphs using ways like comparing, defining, or showing cause and effect, and add helpful features like headings or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts (e.g., essays, oral reports, biographical feature articles) to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write essays or reports that explain a topic clearly by choosing and organizing important information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a style appropriate to audience and purpose (e.g., formal for academic writing).
description_plain: Your child can keep a writing style that fits the audience and purpose, like using formal language for school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their reasons support their main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly in paragraphs and sections.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their opinion and organize reasons and evidence clearly in paragraphs.

description_plain: Your child can write arguments like essays or letters that support their opinions with clear reasons and facts.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another (e.g., a memoir written by and a biography on the same person).

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence an author uses to support their ideas, and tell which ideas are not supported.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, maps) as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can use information from different sources like charts, photos, videos, and words to understand a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.

description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; explain how word choice affects meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and sees how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.

subject: Language Arts

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
