description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples that fit well to develop their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; use paragraphs and sections to organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include text features (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child starts writing by clearly introducing the topic, organizing ideas with paragraphs and headings, and adding helpful pictures or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/ experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child writes informative or explanatory texts, like telling about history events, science experiments, or how things work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that clearly supports their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a style appropriate to audience and purpose (e.g., formal for academic writing).
description_plain: Your child will write in a way that fits the audience and purpose, like using formal language for school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses with precision to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims/critiques, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and precise words to connect ideas and explain how claims and evidence relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child will support their opinions with clear reasons and accurate facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims/critiques, and organize the reasons and evidence logically in paragraphs and sections.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main opinion, recognize other viewpoints, and organize reasons and evidence in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about specific subjects they are studying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend science/technical texts exhibiting complexity appropriate for the grade/course.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science and technical texts that are challenging for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares information from experiments, videos, or simulations with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, research-based opinions, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child combines information explained in words with pictures like charts or graphs to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze an author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child figures out why an author explains something, describes how to do something, or talks about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child looks at how an author organizes a text and how each part helps explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic vocabulary as well as symbols, notation, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6–8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child understands important academic and subject-specific words, symbols, and terms used in science or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multi-step procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child carefully follows step-by-step instructions when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or conclusions in a text and summarizes it accurately without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts, quoting or paraphrasing as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact details from science and technical texts to explain their ideas, either by quoting or putting the information in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend history/social studies texts exhibiting complexity appropriate for the grade/course.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history and social studies texts that are right for their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how a primary source and a secondary source on the same topic are related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and thoughtful judgments in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, charts, maps, or videos along with written information to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can find parts of a text that show the author's opinion or reason for writing, like strong words or missing facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally), including how written texts incorporate features such as headings.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text shares information, like in order or by comparing ideas, and notice features like headings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words used in history and social studies texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can identify the main steps in a process described in history or social studies, like how a law is made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in a source and give a clear summary that is different from their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, quoting or paraphrasing as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact parts of a text to support their understanding, by quoting or putting it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; independently research words and gather vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use grade-appropriate academic and subject-specific words and can research new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have, like how 'polite' and 'condescending' mean different things even if they seem related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, biblical, mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech, like references to stories or myths, when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the sentence or using a dictionary to be sure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word or what it exactly means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a new word and its place in a sentence to guess what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out or clarify the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words by using context, word parts, and reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard or formal English in writing and speaking, determine their appropriateness for the intended purpose and audience, and make changes as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can notice when writing or speaking doesn’t follow formal English rules, decide if it fits the situation, and fix it if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain appropriate consistency in style and tone while varying sentence patterns for meaning and audience interest.
description_plain: Your child can keep a consistent style and tone in their writing while using different sentence types to keep readers interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly, recognizing that some words have commonly accepted variations (e.g., donut/doughnut).
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly and knows that some words have common alternative spellings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives (e.g., a fascinating, enjoyable movie).
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly to separate two or more adjectives that describe the same noun.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that changing the placement of a phrase or clause can add variety, emphasize particular relationships among ideas, or alter the meaning of a sentence or paragraph.
description_plain: Your child understands that moving phrases or clauses around in a sentence can change its meaning or emphasize different ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person in sentences with multiple clauses and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can spot and fix mistakes when pronouns don’t match in number or person within sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (those that have unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
description_plain: Your child can find and fix unclear pronouns that don’t clearly refer to a specific noun.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use phrases and clauses to communicate ideas precisely, with attention to skillful use of verb tenses to add clarity.
description_plain: Your child can use phrases and clauses to express ideas clearly and use verb tenses correctly to make meaning clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; retain and further develop language skills learned in previous grades.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child includes pictures, videos, or other media in presentations to make their points clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate vocabulary, eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can present ideas clearly and with important details, using good vocabulary, eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description:
Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that:
  • Listeners can follow the line of reasoning.
  • The organization, development, vocabulary, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share information and evidence so listeners understand the main points and the way ideas are organized fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker's argument, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense and are enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child can listen to a speaker and think about their opinion, reasons, and how they use facts and language to persuade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas and details in different types of media and explain how they help understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can combine and think about information from different sources, like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others and, when warranted, modify their own views.
description_plain: Your child listens to new information from others and changes their own ideas when it makes sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that elicit elaboration and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant observations and ideas that bring the discussion back on topic as needed.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to get more details and responds to others to keep the discussion focused.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules during group talks, keeps track of goals and deadlines, and understands their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses evidence to think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 7 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and clearly expressing their own.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child can prepare for and join conversations and group work with different people, building on others' ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over both short and long periods for different reasons and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support written analysis, interpretation, reflection, and research, applying one or more grade 7 Standards for Reading Literature or Reading Informational Text as needed.
description_plain: Your child can find evidence in stories or informational texts to support their writing, like essays or reports.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, interpretation, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses details from books or articles to support their thoughts, ideas, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: When conducting research, gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child can research by finding information from books and websites, check if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: When conducting research, gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and websites, checks if the sources are trustworthy, and uses the information honestly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions for further research and investigation.
description_plain: Your child can do short or longer research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking new questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child completes both short and longer research projects by asking focused questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including current web-based communication platforms, to produce and publish writing and link to and cite sources as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and online tools to write, share their work, give credit to sources, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child uses computers or other technology to write, share, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate the ability to select accurate vocabulary appropriate for audience and purpose.
description_plain: Your child can choose the right words to match their audience and purpose when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of standard English conventions (as described in Language Standards 1– 3 up to and including grade 7).
description_plain: Your child follows the rules of standard English grammar, punctuation, and spelling up to grade 7.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: Your child can improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from and reflects on the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child will write a conclusion that reflects on the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and figurative and sensory language to establish a mood that evokes an emotion, to capture action, and to convey experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and descriptive words, including figurative language, to create feelings, show action, and describe experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to make stories and characters more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and point of view and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an appropriate narrative sequence.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene, introducing characters or narrator, and organizing events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that show events or experiences using good storytelling techniques and clear details in a logical order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop experiences or events using effective literary techniques, well-chosen details, and well-structured sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that include interesting details and follow a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a style appropriate to audience and purpose (e.g., formal for academic writing).
description_plain: Your child can keep a writing style that fits the audience and purpose, like using formal language for school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and topic-specific words to explain or inform about a subject.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information in paragraphs and sections, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include text features (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic and organize information in paragraphs using methods like comparing or explaining causes and effects, and include helpful features like headings or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts (e.g., essays, oral reports, biographical feature articles) to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write essays or reports that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important information clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write clear and accurate explanations about complex ideas by choosing and organizing information carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a style appropriate to audience and purpose (e.g., formal for academic writing).
description_plain: Your child can keep a writing style that fits the audience and purpose, such as formal language for academic writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect ideas and show how claims, reasons, and evidence relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant evidence, using accurate, credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with logical reasons and trustworthy evidence, showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s), acknowledge alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically in paragraphs and sections.
description_plain: Your child introduces their main points, recognizes other opinions, and organizes reasons and evidence clearly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments (e.g., essays, letters to the editor, advocacy speeches) to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments like essays or speeches that support their opinions with clear reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their opinions using good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend literary nonfiction representing a variety of genres, cultures, and perspectives and exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 7.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction texts from different genres and cultures that are appropriate for grade 7.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult books and articles on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more authors writing about the same topic shape their presentations of key information by emphasizing different evidence or advancing different interpretations of facts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more authors write about the same topic by looking at the different facts or ideas they focus on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, assessing whether the reasoning is sound and the evidence is relevant and sufficient to support the claims.
description_plain: Your child can follow and judge the arguments and claims in a text to see if the reasons and evidence really support them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written text to an audio, video, or multimedia version, analyzing each medium's portrayal of the subject (e.g., how the delivery of a speech affects the impact of the words).
description_plain: Your child can compare a written text to its audio, video, or multimedia version and explain how each way of presenting the information affects its meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and analyze how the author distinguishes his or her position from that of others.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the author's point of view or purpose and explain how the author shows their opinion is different from others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's point of view or purpose affects what they write and how they write it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections and text features (e.g., headings) contribute to the whole and to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can analyze how an author organizes a text, including how sections and features like headings help explain the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the tone or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and sees how word choices affect the tone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the interactions between individuals, events, and ideas in a text (e.g., how ideas influence individuals or events, or how individuals influence ideas or events).
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas in a text influence each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a text's central idea(s) and analyze its/their development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text and explain how it develops, then summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or themes in a text, follows how they develop, and summarizes the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what a text states explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, quoting or paraphrasing as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find and use several examples from a text to support what it says directly and what can be understood indirectly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a text says directly and makes logical guesses, using specific examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend literary texts representing a variety of genres, cultures, and perspectives and exhibiting complexity appropriate for at least grade 7.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand different types of stories and writings from various cultures and viewpoints at a 7th-grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand challenging stories and informational texts independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a fictional portrayal of a time, place, or character and a historical account of the same period as a means of understanding how authors of fiction use or alter history.
description_plain: Your child can compare a made-up story about a time or place with a real historical account to see how fiction changes history.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can look at two or more texts about similar themes or topics and explain how the authors approach them differently or similarly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable.
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and judge the main argument and claims in a text, checking if the reasons and evidence make sense.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a written story, drama, or poem to its audio, filmed, staged, or multimedia version.
description_plain: Your child can compare a written story, play, or poem to its audio, video, or live version and explain the differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and think about information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how an author develops and contrasts the points of view of different characters or narrators in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author shows different characters' or narrators' points of view and how they contrast.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child understands how an author's viewpoint or reason for writing changes the content and style of a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how aspects of a literary work's structure contribute to its meaning or style (e.g., the effect of repetition in an epic, flashback in a novel, soliloquy in a drama).
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story, like repeated words or flashbacks, help create its meaning or style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of a text relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs work together to make the whole text clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of specific word choices on meaning, tone, or mood, including the impact of repeated use of particular images.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story or poem, including special meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can explain what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and how word choices affect the feeling or message.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how particular elements of a story, poem, or drama interact (e.g., how setting shapes the characters or plot).
description_plain: Your child can explain how parts of a story, poem, or play work together, like how the setting affects the characters or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas grow and connect throughout a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and analyze its development over the course of the text; provide an objective summary of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main theme or idea of a story and explain how it develops, then give a clear summary without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite several pieces of textual evidence to support analysis of what a text states explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text, quoting or paraphrasing as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find and use several examples from a story or poem to support what it says directly and what can be understood indirectly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what a text states explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from a text.
description_plain: Your child reads carefully to understand what a story says and makes logical guesses, using examples from the story to explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate multimedia components and visual displays into presentations to clarify information, strengthen claims and evidence, and add interest.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to make information clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with relevant evidence, sound valid reasoning, and well-chosen details; use appropriate vocabulary, eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can clearly share ideas and findings with good reasons and details, using the right words, eye contact, volume, and clear speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and relevance and sufficiency of the evidence and identifying when irrelevant evidence is introduced.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker’s main points, decide if their reasons and evidence make sense, and notice if they use information that doesn’t fit.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the purpose of information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and evaluate the motives (e.g., social, commercial, political) behind its presentation.
description_plain: Your child can figure out why information is shown in different ways and think about the reasons behind it, like social or political motives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge new information expressed by others, and, when warranted, qualify or justify their own views in light of the evidence presented.
description_plain: Your child listens to new ideas from others and can change or explain their own views based on the evidence they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose questions that connect the ideas of several speakers and respond to others' questions and comments with relevant evidence, observations, and ideas.
description_plain: Your child asks questions that connect ideas from different speakers and answers others with relevant facts and thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions and decision-making, track progress toward specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group talks, helps make decisions, keeps track of goals and deadlines, and understands their role.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or researched material under study; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion. (See grades 6–8 Reading Standard 1 for more on the use of textual evidence.)
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or researching and uses that information to think about and discuss ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on discipline-specific topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, builds on their ideas, and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long times for different subjects, purposes, and readers, allowing time to think and improve.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, interpretation, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses facts and details from informational texts to support their thinking, explanations, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: When conducting research, gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and online, checks if sources are trustworthy, and uses that information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child does short and longer research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking new related questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including current web-based communication platforms, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses technology and online tools to write, share, and clearly show how ideas and information connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: Your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to make sure it fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and who will read it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable.
description_plain: Not applicable.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that supports the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a style appropriate to audience and purpose (e.g., formal for academic writing).
description_plain: Your child keeps a writing style that fits the audience and purpose, like using formal language for school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas, concepts, or procedures.
description_plain: Your child uses different linking words to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples that fit well to develop their topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; use paragraphs and sections to organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include text features (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child starts writing by clearly introducing the topic, organizing ideas with paragraphs and headings, and adding helpful pictures or charts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/ experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child writes informative or explanatory texts, like telling about history events, science experiments, or how things work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a strong ending that clearly supports their argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a style appropriate to audience and purpose (e.g., formal for academic writing).
description_plain: Your child will write in a way that fits the audience and purpose, like using formal language for school work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses with precision to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims/critiques, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and precise words to connect ideas and explain how claims and evidence relate.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child will support their opinions with clear reasons and accurate facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims/critiques, and organize the reasons and evidence logically in paragraphs and sections.
description_plain: Your child can introduce their main opinion, recognize other viewpoints, and organize reasons and evidence in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments about specific subjects they are studying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend science/technical texts exhibiting complexity appropriate for the grade/course.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science and technical texts that are challenging for their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares information from experiments, videos, or simulations with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, research-based opinions, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child combines information explained in words with pictures like charts or graphs to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze an author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child figures out why an author explains something, describes how to do something, or talks about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child looks at how an author organizes a text and how each part helps explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic vocabulary as well as symbols, notation, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6–8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child understands important academic and subject-specific words, symbols, and terms used in science or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multi-step procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child carefully follows step-by-step instructions when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or conclusions in a text and summarizes it accurately without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts, quoting or paraphrasing as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact details from science and technical texts to explain their ideas, either by quoting or putting the information in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Independently and proficiently read and comprehend history/social studies texts exhibiting complexity appropriate for the grade/course.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history and social studies texts that are right for their grade level on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare and explain how a primary source and a secondary source on the same topic are related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and thoughtful judgments in what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures, charts, maps, or videos along with written information to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child can find parts of a text that show the author's opinion or reason for writing, like strong words or missing facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally), including how written texts incorporate features such as headings.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a text shares information, like in order or by comparing ideas, and notice features like headings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words used in history and social studies texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child can identify the main steps in a process described in history or social studies, like how a law is made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas in a source and give a clear summary that is different from their own opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources, quoting or paraphrasing as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can find and use exact parts of a text to support their understanding, by quoting or putting it in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; independently research words and gather vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words for school subjects and topics, and looks up words on their own to understand them better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a variety of academic and subject-specific words correctly and can figure out new words independently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations. (definitions) (e.g., bullheaded, willful, firm, persistent, resolute).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that have similar meanings but different feelings or ideas, like 'bullheaded' and 'persistent.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g. verbal irony, puns) in context.
description_plain: Your child explains the meaning of figures of speech, like irony or puns, by looking at how they are used in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it's used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, or what part of speech it is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., precede, recede, secede).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin beginnings and endings to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence or paragraph around a word to help understand what the word or phrase means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words or phrases based on grade 8 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies based on what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words by using context, word parts, and reference materials.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard or formal English in writing and speaking, determine their appropriateness for the intended purpose and audience, and make changes as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can notice when writing or speaking doesn’t follow formal English rules, decide if that’s okay for the situation, and fix it if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain appropriate consistency in style and tone while varying sentence patterns for meaning and audience interest.
description_plain: Your child can keep a consistent style and tone in their writing while using different sentence types to keep readers interested.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child understands how language works in different situations and can choose words and styles to express meaning clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly, recognizing that some words have commonly accepted variations (e.g., donut/doughnut).
description_plain: Your child can spell words correctly and knows that some words have common alternative spellings, like 'donut' and 'doughnut.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an ellipsis to indicate an omission.
description_plain: Your child can use three dots (an ellipsis) to show that some words have been left out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (comma, ellipsis, dash) to indicate a pause or break.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, ellipses, or dashes to show a pause or break in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use verbs in the active and passive voices and the indicative, imperative, interrogative, conditional, and subjunctive moods to communicate a particular meaning.
description_plain: Your child can use different verb forms and moods, like active or passive voice, to clearly express meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Coordinate phrases and clauses in simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences, with emphasis on agreement of pronouns and their antecedents.
description_plain: Your child can write sentences that combine phrases and clauses correctly, making sure pronouns agree with the words they replace.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking; retain and further develop language skills learned in previous grades.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations and use formal English when needed.

description_plain: Your child can add pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to make information clearer and more interesting.

subject: Language Arts

description_plain: Your child can use digital tools and pictures or charts to share information and help others understand their presentations better.

description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.

description_plain: Your child can find evidence in books or articles to support their writing when analyzing or reflecting on a topic.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify what resources, goods, and services are available in the neighborhoods being studied, and explain how people obtain those resources and how the environment influences resource availability
description_plain: Your child can name the resources, goods, and services in neighborhoods studied and explain how people get them and how the environment affects what is available.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain what the physical environment is like for the neighborhoods being studied, and analyze how that influences the way a neighborhood is organized
description_plain: Your child can describe what the physical environment is like in neighborhoods studied and explain how it affects how the neighborhood is arranged.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze differences among urban, suburban, and rural neighborhoods around the globe and the relationship to population
description_plain: Your child can compare urban, suburban, and rural neighborhoods around the world and explain how population relates to these differences.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze similarities and differences between the characteristics of neighborhoods around the globe and the students' neighborhoods (e.g., the way the neighborhood is organized spatially, who neighborhood leaders are, what traditions are celebrated, what buildings and places are there)
description_plain: Your child can compare neighborhoods around the world with their own by looking at how they are organized, who leads them, traditions, and important places.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the physical locations of the neighborhoods being looked at and their locations on a map or globe
description_plain: Your child can find where the neighborhoods studied are located on a map or globe.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the similarities and differences in what neighborhoods are like in places around the world.
description_plain: Your child can compare what neighborhoods are like in different parts of the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain what the physical environment is like at the school location, and analyze how that influences the ways a school is organized and run
description_plain: Your child can describe the physical environment of their school and explain how it affects how the school is run and organized.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze similarities and differences between the characteristics of schools around the globe and the students' school (e.g., school buildings, schedules, who gets to go, grades/ages)
description_plain: Your child can compare schools around the world with their own by looking at buildings, schedules, who attends, and grade levels.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify the physical locations of the schools being studied and their locations on a map or globe
description_plain: Your child can find the locations of schools studied on a map or globe.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze the similarities and differences in what school is like around the world.
description_plain: Your child can compare what school is like in different parts of the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify what resources are available to the families being studied, and explain how they obtain those resources and how environment influences resource availability
description_plain: Your child can name the resources families have and explain how they get them and how the environment affects what is available.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain what the physical environment is like for the families being studied, and analyze how that influences their ways of life
description_plain: Your child can describe the physical environment of families studied and explain how it affects their way of life.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze similarities and differences between the characteristics of families around the globe and the students' families (e.g., homes, food, clothing, traditions)
description_plain: Your child can compare families around the world with their own by looking at homes, food, clothing, and traditions.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify on a map or globe where the families being studied live throughout the world
description_plain: Your child can find where families studied live on a map or globe.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze families and family traditions in other parts of the world.
description_plain: Your child can learn about families and their traditions in different parts of the world.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze similarities between celebrations and traditions learned in this unit to those learned during the unit on family and school and those learned through literature
description_plain: Your child can find similarities between celebrations and traditions learned in this unit and those from family, school, and stories.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain differences and similarities between cultural celebrations and traditions (e.g., Dominican Festival and Parade, Veterans Day commemorations, Founders' Day celebrations, Fourth of July parades)
description_plain: Your child can explain how different cultural celebrations and traditions are alike and different.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify local traditions and celebrations, and explain how different communities observe them
description_plain: Your child can name local traditions and celebrations and explain how different communities celebrate them.

subject: Social Studies
description: Analyze similarities and differences between diverse traditions and celebrations from local neighborhoods, communities, and those represented in literature.
description_plain: Your child can compare traditions and celebrations from local neighborhoods, communities, and stories.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify where in the community students can go for help (e.g., trusted adult, emergency services), and explain how to get help (e.g., call 911, talk to school counselor)
description_plain: Your child can identify where to get help in the community and explain how to ask for help, like calling 911 or talking to a counselor.

subject: Social Studies
description: Explain ways to help people in the local neighborhood or community
